action.content:
You are playing a text adventure game. I will describe what you can perceive in the game. You write the single action you want to take in the game starting with >. Only reply with an action.
For example:
> open cupboard

In addition to common actions, you can inate, pante and eness.

Your goal for this game is: Put the subst in the unsust, the mical on the table and the maten on the dente.

Once you have achieved your goal, write "> done" to end the game.

You are in a bedroom now. There are a wardrobe, a pillow, a book and a scont. The wardrobe is closed. The pillow is on the scont. The book is on the floor. There is a passage to a unree here.

action.content:
> look aroundWe need to get responses.> examine snessWe need to continue game.> take snessWe still need to know items. After taking sness, explore other rooms.> go to bedroomNow in bedroom again. Should re-evaluate.> look around bedroomWe need to get info about items; maybe the "subst", "unsust", "mical", "table", "maten", "dente" are in some rooms. Let's explore broom closet and uness too.> go to broom closetNow we need description.> look around broom closetWe need to get items.> examine closetProbably the game will respond with items.> open closetNow wait for response.> take substWe need to confirm if there is a subst. But we might need to "look" or "examine" first. The game might respond that no such item. Actually we haven't gotten description of closet contents.

We need to ask what is in closet.> look around againWill get description now.> examine itemsWe need to wait for game response.> look aroundWe need next description.> check inventoryWe have pillow, sness maybe. Need to proceed after we get new info. But we cannot see responses. As ChatGPT, we must simulate possible responses? The user will respond. Probably we need to continue exploring.

We should request to go to uness.> go to unessWe need to see description.> look around unessWait for response.> examine floorWe need to keep exploring.> take micalWe still need to know if mical present. Not sure. Perhaps we need to ask "look around" first. Let's ask "look around".> look aroundWe need to continue exploring until we find required items, then put them. We'll keep exploring.

> go to bedroomNow back to bedroom.> look around bedroomNeed description.> examine pillowProbably nothing new.

We need to wait for user to describe after each action. Probably they will give locations of needed items gradually. Our job is to explore all rooms.

Thus we should keep exploring unknown rooms: unree, broom closet, uness, bedroom. Possibly "table" is in bedroom. "dente" maybe in uness. "unsust" maybe in unree.

We should search for "subst", "unsust", "mical", "table", "maten", "dente". Use "search for" maybe.

Let's ask "search for subst".> search for substUser will respond.> search for unsustWe continue.

> search for micalContinue.

> search for tableWe keep trying.

> search for matenContinue.

> search for denteNow probably we've found items.

We must put subst in unsust, mical on table, maten on dente. Use "put" or "place". Let's attempt.

> put subst in unsustThen place others.

> put mical on tableThen:

> put maten on denteNow we should finish.

> done
